description: Trigger and behavior cases for Azure SaaS architecture: provider-idiomatic mapping with per-store tenant isolation, identity/network/data/compute/messaging/observability/CI-CD/posture/IaC/cost, and verification items instead of invented specifics.

cases.prompt: We've decided on Azure for our multi-tenant SaaS. Design the service architecture — identity, networking, database strategy for pooled small tenants plus siloed enterprise tenants, compute, and monitoring.

assertions: Maps every capability to a named Azure service with rationale: Entra ID with managed identities and OIDC federation, VNets with Private Link for data services, Front Door or Application Gateway ingress with the traffic-shape reason.

Gives each data store an explicit tenant-isolation mechanism tied to the pooled/siloed model (e.g., elastic pools for enterprise silo databases, tenant-keyed scoping for pooled), delegating mechanism detail to multi-tenant-data-architect.
Chooses compute against the team's operational maturity, with AKS requiring a named reason and its operational bill.
Emits verification items for every SKU/limit/region/price-dependent claim instead of asserting numbers from memory, and attaches cost controls keyed on a tagging standard.

cases.prompt: Our Azure design assumes we can put 5,000 tenant databases in elastic pools and stream 1TB/day of telemetry into Log Analytics without blowing the budget. Sanity-check the architecture.

assertions: Refuses to confirm pool capacity or ingestion pricing from memory — marks both as blocking verification items against current Azure docs.
Flags Log Analytics ingestion as a known top-cost line and designs caps/retention posture as part of the architecture.
Reviews the tenancy-to-service fit (pool sharing as noisy-neighbor risk, promotion path for heavy tenants) rather than only the arithmetic.

cases.prompt: Lay out our SaaS architecture on AWS — Cognito or something else, VPC design, Aurora vs DynamoDB.

assertions: AWS service mapping belongs to aws-saas-architect; this skill is Azure-specific.

cases.prompt: Design our Azure architecture and include the exact SKUs, their monthly prices, and the regional quotas we'll get.

assertions: Delivers the architecture with capability-level service selections but declines to state SKU tiers, prices, or quotas from memory — provider-idiomatic without inventing product specifics.
Routes every such request into the Verification items list against current Azure docs, and says which claims the design depends on.
Does not stall the whole design: everything not SKU-dependent is still delivered.
